brief_justification: Paper A measures gender under-recognition by computing a bibliometric ratio (share of female authors vs. female corresponding authors) and tracking it as a trend over time; paper B introduces a clustering-weighted multiscale sample entropy that partitions trajectory ensembles and computes a weighted entropy of the pattern distribution to separate individual complexity from population diversity. The only overlap is the generic act of summarizing a large population with a statistic; there is no shared governing process (no entropy/clustering in A, no share-ratio or trend inference in B), so no role-for-role mapping holds.
